# The Great Traffic War: How Google Gemini Seized 20% of the AI Market and Challenged ChatGPT’s Hegemony
By:[TokenRing AI] 
January 26, 2026 at 15:11 PM EST
![Photo for article] 
In a dramatic shift that has reshaped the artificial intelligence landscape over the past twelve months, Alphabet Inc. ([NASDAQ: GOOGL]) has successfully leveraged its massive Android ecosystem to break the near-monopoly once held by OpenAI. As of January 26, 2026, new industry data confirms that Google Gemini has surged to a commanding 20% share of global LLM (Large Language Model) traffic, marking the most significant competitive challenge to ChatGPT since the AI boom began. This rapid ascent from a mere 5% market share a year ago signals a pivotal moment in the "Traffic War," as the battle for AI dominance moves from standalone web interfaces to deep system-level integration.
The implications of this surge are profound for the tech industry. While ChatGPT remains the individual market leader, its absolute dominance is waning under the pressure of Google’s "ambient AI" strategy. By making Gemini the default intelligence layer for billions of devices, Google has transformed the generative AI market from a destination-based experience into a seamless, omnipresent utility. This shift has forced a strategic "Code Red" at OpenAI and its primary backer, Microsoft Corp. ([NASDAQ: MSFT]), as they scramble to defend their early lead against the sheer distributional force of the Android and Chrome ecosystems.
## The Engine of Growth: Technical Integration and Gemini 3

# Google Sees Surge in Sales of Gemini AI Models
By[PYMNTS] |January 20, 2026
[**] [**] [**] [**] |******
[Google] has reportedly seen a surge in sales of its Gemini artificial intelligence (AI) models.
## Get the Full Story
Complete the form to unlock this article and enjoy unlimited free access to all PYMNTS content —no additional logins required.

Δ
This uptick[indicates an improvement] in the quality of those models, according to Seeking Alpha, which itself cited a paywalled report by The Information Tuesday (Jan. 20).
This could add more revenue from Google Cloud’s core business of server sales, as the money customers shell out for AI often leads to more spending on other Google products, the report added, citing a source with knowledge of Google’s sales.
The report notes that Google sells access to its[AI models] via Google Cloud via an application programming interface (API). Requests sent to the Gemini API more than doubled between March and August of last year, the report added, citing internal data from Google.
The Information adds that a major challenge still facing Google is convincing business customers to pay for the complex software it has created to run on top of its AI models. Sources said this software gives Google a way to boost the profit margins on its artificial intelligence business.
In other Google AI news, PYMNTS wrote last week about the company’s[new effort] to embed[Walmart] and[Sam’s Club’s] massive inventory directly into Gemini using Google’s[Universal Commerce Protocol].

# Google Gemini AI Adoption Soars: 85B API Calls, 34% Cloud Revenue Jump
Google's Gemini AI has surged in adoption, with API calls doubling to 85 billion and 8 million enterprise subscribers, driving a 34% cloud revenue jump and Alphabet's $4 trillion valuation via partnerships like Apple. This positions Gemini as a leader in AI integration for business operations and user engagement.
![] 
**Google Gemini AI Adoption Soars: 85B API Calls, 34% Cloud Revenue Jump**
**Written by John Marshall**
Monday, January 19, 2026
# Gemini’s Ascendance: Google’s AI Powerhouse Fuels Unprecedented Corporate Surge
In the fast-evolving realm of artificial intelligence, Google’s Gemini model has emerged as a formidable force, driving remarkable growth in business adoption and sales. Recent data reveals that API calls for Gemini more than doubled to 85 billion by August, signaling a dramatic uptick in usage among developers and enterprises. This surge underscores how Gemini is not just keeping pace but outstripping competitors in the race to integrate AI into everyday business operations. As companies across sectors seek efficient tools for automation, content generation, and data analysis, Gemini’s robust capabilities have positioned it as a go-to solution.
The enterprise version of Gemini has seen its subscriber base expand to 8 million, a testament to its appeal in professional settings. Businesses are leveraging Gemini for tasks ranging from code generation to customer service enhancements, finding it more reliable and versatile than earlier iter...",

## Google Gemini Surge Reshapes Cloud Computing Market
Developers are hammering Google’s Gemini models at unprecedented scale. However, the bigger story sits within the underlying Cloud Computing infrastructure powering that demand. According to The Information, Gemini API requests reached about 35 billion in March 2025. Consequently, that figure ballooned to roughly 85 billion in August.
Meanwhile, Gemini Enterprise subscriptions hit eight million across roughly 1,500 companies, according to the same leak. Moreover, Google claims active users on AI Studio and the Gemini API grew more than 200 percent this year. Such acceleration underscores Google Cloud’s bid to outpace rivals, monetize models, and justify massive capital expenditure. In contrast, skeptics warn that profitability remains elusive once research spending is considered. Therefore, executives and investors alike watch these metrics closely to gauge sustainable returns. This article unpacks the numbers, examines drivers, and assesses future implications for enterprise architects and product leaders.
![Cloud Computing infrastructure data center servers and technician monitoring] Data centers underpin the Cloud Computing power surge reshaping business operations. ## Gemini API Usage Surge
Firstly, the raw figures reveal explosive API Growth not often seen in enterprise software history. According to The Information, Gemini API requests reached about 35 billion in March 2025. Subsequently, that tally climbed to roughly 85 billion by August.
Consider these headline numbers:
* 143 percent request increase within five months
* Average daily calls surpassed 2.7 billion by late summer
* Eight million Gemini Enterprise seats sold across 1,500 organizations
* Gemini 2.5 reportedly profitable on operating costs
